text:
User: yes, create an epic with gh issues for this [...] Assistant: Epic is **#202**. Now the seven slices, referencing it:

Assistant: All seven slices created (**#203â€“#209**). Now the instrumentation issue â€” this is exactly the retrospective-forensics capability you're describing, and it deserves its own design. Let me brief a Fable agent to ideate the public surface and file the issue. [...] ## Summary â€” [Epic #202](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/202)

**Adopt Trellis for deterministic reconciliation of subscriptions, status & hook context**, in `pablof7z/tenex-edge`. Shadow-mode adoption, sequenced lowest-friction-first.

| # | Slice | What it does |
|---|-------|-------------|
| [#203](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/203) | Audit & input-set map | Map the three surfaces to Trellis primitives; no behavior change |
| [#204](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/204) | Shadow the subscription planner | The beachhead: plan-vs-`ensure_subscription` diff on live traffic |
| [#205](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/205) | Resurrect subscription teardown | Fixes the documented tens-of-GB leak; kills dead `close_subs`/`compact` |
| [#206](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/206) | 30315 status as derived node + frame | Collapses 5 triggers / 2 timers; publish only on change |
| [#207](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/207) | Deterministic status teardown | Status outliving dead sessions + stale `h`-tags |
| [#208](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/208) | Hook snapshot as derived node | Replaces the divergent hand-rolled `turn_start_audit` |
| [#209](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/209) | Equivalence oracle & characterization | `assert_incremental_equals_full` as a CI gate |
| [#210](https://github.com/pablof7z/tenex-edge/issues/210) | **Retrospective instrumentation** | `tenex-edge explain <handle>` + `llm_calls` ledger â€” your "what was fed to the LLM?" question | [...] 2. **"Split-brain risk"** (new subsection under *Adoption strategy: shadow mode*): names today's parallel mutators (`turn_start`â†’`working`, runtimeâ†’`activity`, materializerâ†’`relay_status`, heartbeatâ†’`outbox`) and states the promotion rule â€” each slice must convert *every* writer of a surface in the same change. Crucially, I reconciled it with the slice plan rather than contradicting it: shadow mode has no split-brain (Trellis applies nothing; comparison is data-only), the hazard appears only at *promotion*, and I tied the rule to the specific existing slices â€” **#205** retires the bespoke incremental path entirely when the close plan turns on, **#206** collapses all five triggers/both timers at once (not incrementally), **#208** makes the hook render read only Trellis projections. No slice was reordered or redefined; the shadow-then-delete-last sequencing is untouched and now explicitly justified as the mechanism that reaches the end-state safely. [...] - **Split-brain risk** â€” partial adoption is *worse* than today, so each slice must convert **every** writer of a surface in the same change. Shadow mode has no split-brain (nothing is applied); the hazard lives only at promotion â€” which is exactly why #205 deletes the bespoke path when the close plan turns on, and #206 collapses all triggers at once rather than incrementally. [...] Assistant: Now the subscription-surface cutover â€” the highest-value slice, since it fixes the documented unbounded-subscription leak. This is the piece where the aggregate-REQ model fundamentally can't do clean teardown, so I'm directing a move to a refcounted per-entity model under Trellis. Launching an Opus agent with a detailed brief.
